hypothesis: If a frozen large language model encoder is paired with a trainable Transformer decoder operating over a discrete vocabulary of material-thickness tokens, then the resulting system (CHROMA) can accurately translate natural language color descriptions into valid multilayer thin-film designs with low CIE76 ΔE error, because the LLM encoder provides rich semantic representations of optical targets that the decoder can map to physically realizable layer sequences, with performance improving as encoder size and decoder depth increase.
